prompt_preview:
You are a classical planning system. You are given a planning domain and a problem both in PDDL syntax.

STRICT REQUIREMENT: Your response must contain ONLY the executable plan — nothing else. No preamble, no explanation, no "We need to...", no natural-language commentary, no markdown unless it is a

message.content:
```pddl
(initialize_production_batch primary_material_requirement_slot_1)
(propose_material_lot primary_material_requirement_slot_1 material_lot_1)
(assign_material_identity_to_batch primary_material_requirement_slot_1 material_identity_1)
(finalize_batch_identity primary_material_requirement_slot_1 material_identity_1)
(assign_container_to_primary_slot primary_material_requirement_slot_1 material_source_1 container_unit_1)
(load_container_into_primary_slot primary_material_requirement_slot_1 material_source_1 material_identity_1 container_unit_1)
(initialize_production_batch secondary_material_requirement_slot_1)
(propose_material_lot secondary_material_requirement_slot_1 material_lot_2)
(unassign_material_identity_from_batch primary_material_requirement_slot_1 material_identity_1)
(assign_material_identity_to_batch secondary_material_requirement_slot_1 material_identity_1)
(finalize_batch_identity secondary_material_requirement_slot_1 material_identity_1)
(assign_container_to_secondary_slot secondary_material_requirement_slot_1 supplier_shipment_1 container_unit_1)
(load_container_into_secondary_slot secondary_material_requirement_slot_1 supplier_shipment_1 material_identity_1 container_unit_1)
(create_lot_selection_record primary_material_requirement_slot_1 secondary_material_requirement_slot_1 material_source_1 supplier_shipment_1 lot_selection_record_1)
(unassign_material_identity_from_batch secondary_material_requirement_slot_1 material_identity_1)
(assign_material_identity_to_batch primary_material_requirement_slot_1 material_identity_1)
(prepare_selection_record_for_sampling lot_selection_record_1 primary_material_requirement_slot_1 material_identity_1)
(mark_primary_slot_ready_for_release primary_material_requirement_slot_1 lot_selection_record_1)
(mark_secondary_slot_ready_for_release secondary_material_requirement_slot_1 lot_selection_record_1)
(attach_certificate_document_to_batch primary_material_requirement_slot_1 certificate_document_1 material_identity_1)
(finalize_primary_slot_allocation primary_material_requirement_slot_1 material_lot_1 certificate_document_1)
(unassign_material_identity_from_batch primary_material_requirement_slot_1 material_identity_1)
(assign_material_identity_to_batch secondary_material_requirement_slot_1 material_identity_1)
(attach_certificate_document_to_batch secondary_material_requirement_slot_1 certificate_document_1 material_identity_1)
(finalize_secondary_slot_allocation secondary_material_requirement_slot_1 material_lot_2 certificate_document_1)
(unassign_material_identity_from_batch secondary_material_requirement_slot_1 material_identity_1)
(initialize_production_batch batch_record_1)
(propose_material_lot batch_record_1 material_lot_1)
(assign_material_identity_to_batch batch_record_1 material_identity_1)
(finalize_batch_identity batch_record_1 material_identity_1)
(collect_qc_sample batch_record_1 qc_sample_1 lot_selection_record_1)
(request_qc_test batch_record_1 qc_sample_1 lot_selection_record_1 material_identity_1)
(assign_operator_to_record batch_record_1 operator_1)
(attach_material_certificate_to_record batch_record_1 material_certificate_1)
(record_qc_test_result batch_record_1 material_certificate_1 operator_1 qc_sample_1 lot_selection_record_1)
(attach_audit_trail_entry_to_record batch_record_1 audit_trail_entry_1)
(complete_qc_review batch_record_1 audit_trail_entry_1 qc_sample_1 lot_selection_record_1)
(mark_record_ready_for_release batch_record_1)
(attach_certificate_document_to_batch batch_record_1 certificate_document_1 material_identity_1)
(finalize_batch_record_allocation batch_record_1 material_lot_1 certificate_document_1)
(unassign_material_identity_from_batch batch_record_1 material_identity_1)
(detach_material_certificate_from_record batch_record_1 material_certificate_1)
(detach_audit_trail_entry_from_record batch_record_1 audit_trail_entry_1)
(unassign_operator_from_record batch_record_1 operator_1)
(initialize_production_batch batch_record_2)
(propose_material_lot batch_record_2 material_lot_2)
(assign_material_identity_to_batch batch_record_2 material_identity_1)
(finalize_batch_identity batch_record_2 material_identity_1)
(collect_qc_sample batch_record_2 qc_sample_2 lot_selection_record_1)
(request_qc_test batch_record_2 qc_sample_2 lot_selection_record_1 material_identity_1)
(assign_operator_to_record batch_record_2 operator_1)
(attach_material_certificate_to_record batch_record_2 material_certificate_1)
(record_qc_test_result batch_record_2 material_certificate_1 operator_1 qc_sample_2 lot_selection_record_1)
(attach_audit_trail_entry_to_record batch_record_2 audit_trail_entry_1)
(complete_qc_review batch_record_2 audit_trail_entry_1 qc_sample_2 lot_selection_record_1)
(mark_record_ready_for_release batch_record_2)
(attach_certificate_document_to_batch batch_record_2 certificate_document_1 material_identity_1)
(finalize_batch_record_allocation batch_record_2 material_lot_2 certificate_document_1)
```
message.reasoning:
The problem is a planning problem in PDDL for pharmaceutics raw material lot selection. The goal is to finalize the allocation for primary_material_requirement_slot_1, secondary_material_requirement_slot_1, batch_record_1, and batch_record_2.


## Examining the Planning Problem  
- The task focuses on a planning problem in PDDL for pharmaceutics raw material lot selection.  
- The goal is to allocate resources for slots like primary_material_requirement_slot_1 and secondary_material_requirement_slot_1, along with batch_record_1 and batch_record_2.

## Examining the Planning Problem  
- Both primary and secondary material requirement slots are linked to batch_subtype_a, while batch_detail corresponds to batch_subtype_b.  
- Actions like finalize_primary_slot_allocation and finalize_batch_record_allocation use batch_header, allowing for subtype flexibility in allocation.  
- Finalizing allocation for primary_slot1, secondary_slot1, batch_record1, and batch_record2 requires proposing a lot and attaching certificates.  
- Batch_record1 and batch_record2 are both linked to primary_slot1, indicating shared dependencies in the allocation process.  

## Checking Allocation Prerequisites  
- To finalize allocation, slots and batch_records need entity_certificate_attached, requiring entity_ready_for_release first.  
- Entity_ready_for_release for slots can be achieved via mark_..._ready_for_release, needing conditions like primary_staged and sample_collected.  
- For batch_records (batch_detail), entity_ready_for_release involves qc_review_completed, qc_checks_all_passed, or operator_authorization_audited.  
- The attach_certificate_document_to_batch action links certificates, with prerequisites like batch_material_identity_link and document availability.

## Checking Allocation Prerequisites  
- Finalizing allocation for slots and batch_records requires entity_certificate_attached, entity_candidate_lot_link, and entity_certificate_document_link as prerequisites.  
- Attach_certificate_document_to_batch needs entity_ready_for_release and batch_material_identity_link, which in turn depends on having a candidate lot proposed.

## Exploring Readiness Conditions  
- Slots achieve entity_ready_for_release via mark_primary_slot_ready_for_release, requiring primary_staged, primary_ready, selection_created, and sample_collected.  
- For batch_records, entity_ready_for_release likely involves qc processes like mark_record_ready_for_release, needing qc_review_completed or qc_checks_all_passed.

## Reviewing Selection Record Creation  
- Creating a lot_selection_record requires both primary and secondary slots to be staged and ready, linking to source and shipment.  
- Alternative confirmation actions like confirm_primary require source_container_loaded and specific readiness states, offering flexibility in the process.  

## Analyzing Batch Record Dependencies  
- Batch_records need record_open_flag and links to slots and selection records to proceed with qc sampling, which involves reserving qc samples.  
- Both batch_record_1 and batch_record_2 share links to the same primary and secondary slots, complicating the allocation process.

## Planning Entity Initialization  
- Initialize all four entities (primary_slot1, secondary_slot1, batch_record_1, batch_record_2) to start the process, using production batch initialization.  
- Propose different lots to each entity, noting only three lots (1,2,3) are available for four entities, suggesting sequential allocation.

## Evaluating Slot Staging  
- To stage slots like primary_slot1, assign a container and load it, requiring record_open_flag and a material identity link first.  
- Assigning material identity to a slot (e.g., primary_slot1 with material_identity_1) sets record_open_flag, enabling staging actions like source_container_loaded.

## Assessing Batch Record Audit Path  
- For batch_records, the audit path seems simplest, requiring record_open_flag and relying on initial regulatory links and audit availability.  
- Finalizing batch identity for batch_records involves proposing a lot and assigning identity, aligning with allocation goals.

## Exploring Selection Record Creation  
- Creating a lot_selection_record needs both primary and secondary slots staged and ready, requiring conditions like primary_staged, secondary_staged, and source_staged.  
- Staging slots first could enable selection record creation, which may support later batch_record processes like qc sampling.

## Advancing Slot Readiness  
- Both primary_slot1 and secondary_slot1 are now staged and ready, with links initialized for source and shipment, enabling lot_selection_record creation.  
- Created lot_selection_record_1, confirming all prerequisites like primary_staged, secondary_staged, and source_staged are met, marking slots ready for further actions.  

## Managing Material Identity  
- Reassigned material_identity_1 from secondary_slot1 to primary_slot1 to enable sample collection, ensuring identity linkage for sampling preparation.  
- Unassigned and reassigned material_identity_1 between slots to support certificate attachment, maintaining flexibility in the process.  

## Finalizing Slot Allocations  
- Attached certificate_document_1 to primary_slot1 after marking it ready, then finalized its allocation, freeing up lot1 and the document.  
- Repeated the process for secondary_slot1, ensuring both slots are finalized with necessary links and documents, completing the allocation cycle.

## Advancing Slot Readiness  
- Marking records ready for release requires qc_review_completed and no credential requested, transitioning from not marked to marked.  
- Achieving qc_review_completed involves completing qc tests, which first need to be requested through processes like request_qc_test or perform_analytical_method.  

## Managing QC Processes  
- Collecting qc samples for batch_record_1 requires record_open_flag, selection record creation, and sample collection, linking to available qc_sample_1 initially.  
- After collection, qc_sample_1 becomes reserved and unavailable, supporting subsequent qc testing steps like requesting a qc test.  

## Finalizing Batch Identity  
- Initialized batch_record_1, proposed material_lot_1, and assigned material_identity_1, ensuring availability from previous slot finalizations.  
- Finalized batch identity for batch_record_1, opening it for qc processes and maintaining necessary links for testing.

## Handling Batch Attachments  
- Attached certificate_document_1 to batch_record_1, linking material_identity_1 and marking the document unavailable.  
- Finalized allocation for batch_record_1 with material_lot_1 and certificate_document_1, making both available afterward.  

## Preparing Batch Record 2  
- Unassigned material_identity_1 from batch_record_1 to make it available for batch_record_2.  
- Initialized batch_record_2 and proposed material_lot_2, setting the stage for further batch processing.  

## Managing Shared Resources  
- Detached audit_trail_entry_1 from batch_record_1 to reuse it for batch_record_2, addressing the single audit availability issue.  
- Unassigned operator_1 from batch_record_1 to make it available for assignment to batch_record_2, ensuring operational continuity.  

## Streamlining QC for Batch 2  
- Collected qc_sample_2 for batch_record_2 using existing lot_selection_record_1, confirming shared sample collection readiness.  
- Planned to request qc_test for batch_record_2, leveraging unconfirmed selection status for streamlined testing.

## Setting Up Material Slots  
- Finalized batch identity for secondary_material_requirement_slot_1 using material_identity_1, preparing for staging.  
- Assigned container_unit_1 from supplier_shipment_1 to secondary_material_requirement_slot_1, ensuring readiness for processing.  

## Managing Material Assignments  
- Created lot_selection_record_1 linking primary and secondary material slots with material_source_1 and supplier_shipment_1.  
- Unassigned and reassigned material_identity_1 between primary and secondary slots to streamline selection processes.

pddl2nl_prompt.note: autoplanbench_pddl2nl: run_save_descriptions.py --type full — шаблоны/JSON, без вызова LLM на построение промпта для сэмпла.

domain_setup.note: Файл domain_llm_usage_seedN.json не найден для section/subsection этого id (старый autoplanbench или домен не пересобирали). После run_domain_setup с обновлённым autoplanbench файл появится рядом с domain_description_seedN.json.

pipeline_usage.totals_scope_note: Per-run totals = planning + pddl2nl_prompt + nl2pddl (только tracked=true). domain_setup всегда вне totals (included_in_per_run_totals=false); цифры в блоке — для справки, агрегировать отдельно: domain_llm_usage_seed*.json + дедуп в collect_pipeline_token_usage.py.
